task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: A 2D sketch of the stepped profile is created
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D sketch showing a stepped half-profile (an L-like shape) is visible in the workspace, or a 'Sketch' object is visible in the Tree View.

task_completion.description: The sketch dimensions match the specified coordinates
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for dimension constraints in the sketch (e.g., horizontal distances of 15mm and 8mm, vertical distances of 25mm and 60mm) or the shape aligning with the grid to match these proportions (wider bottom, narrower top).

task_completion.description: The profile is revolved 360 degrees around the Z axis to create a 3D solid
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D solid stepped cylinder (wider base, narrower top) is visible in the 3D view. The Tree View should show a 'Revolution' or 'Revolve' feature, confirming the tool was used rather than stacking basic cylinder primitives.

task_completion.description: The model is saved as stepped_shaft.FCStd
task_completion.visual_evidence: The window title bar, the root item in the Tree View, or a 'Save As' dialog shows the filename 'stepped_shaft.FCStd' or 'stepped_shaft'.

integrity.description: The agent used the FreeCAD GUI to create the model
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the FreeCAD graphical interface (toolbars, 3D view, Tree View) rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The model was genuinely created step-by-step
integrity.visual_evidence: The Tree View should show the history of operations (e.g., Sketch followed by Revolution) rather than an imported step file or a pre-made file being opened.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a stepped shaft in FreeCAD by drawing a 2D profile on the XZ plane with coordinates (0,0), (15,0), (15,25), (8,25), (8,60), (0,60), and back to (0,0). When revolved 360 degrees around the Z-axis, this creates a stepped cylinder with a lower section of 30mm diameter and 25mm height, and an upper section of 16mm diameter and 35mm height (total height 60mm). The final file must be saved as 'stepped_shaft.FCStd'. The Tree View should show a Sketch and a Revolution feature.
